name: ObjectStatus
description: Displays a status text with semantic color coding — used for approval status, workflow state, or any condition that has positive/negative/warning/neutral meaning. Always use ObjectStatus instead of plain Text for status columns — it carries semantic color that plain Text cannot provide.

props.name: state
props.description: Semantic color of the status. Success=green, Warning=amber, Error=red, Information=blue, None=neutral gray. Never hardcode colors — always use state.

props.name: icon
props.description: Optional icon shown before the text. Use SAP icon URI format: 'sap-icon://accept'. Provides a non-color indicator for accessibility.

props.name: title
props.description: Label shown above the status text. Used when ObjectStatus is displayed in a header content area (KPI-style). Omit when used in table cells.

props.name: active
props.description: When true, the ObjectStatus is clickable and fires a press event. Use for interactive status navigation.

props.name: inverted
props.description: Inverts the color scheme — colored background with white text. Use sparingly, only for high-emphasis status indicators.

events.name: press
events.description: Fired when an active ObjectStatus is pressed.

notes: Always use ObjectStatus for status columns in tables — never use sap.m.Text for semantic states.
State must always be set alongside text — color alone is insufficient for accessibility.
In table cells (ColumnListItem), omit the title property — it is only for header content area usage.
Provide a non-color indicator (icon) when possible for color-blind users.
State color alone is not sufficient — always pair with text or icon.
When used in a table, the column header provides context for screen readers.
For critical statuses, consider adding an icon alongside the state color.

examples.title: Status column in a table row
whenToUse: Status columns in List Report and Worklist tables
Approval status on Object Page header (Approved, Rejected, Pending)
Any field where the value has semantic positive/negative/warning meaning
whenNotToUse: Do not use sap.m.Text for status fields — use ObjectStatus to carry semantic color
Do not hardcode status colors with CSS — always use the state property
